name: Paralympic Movement

colors.symbolism: peace, neutrality, and the field on which the Agitos symbol is displayed
one of the three Agitos crescents — the three colours (red, blue, green) are the three most common in the world's national flags

description: A white field charged at the centre with the Agitos — three asymmetrical crescents in red, blue, and green encircling a common point.
history: The Paralympic flag carrying the current Agitos symbol was adopted in 2003 (designed by Scott Givens for the 2004 Athens Paralympic Games) and has been the official symbol of the International Paralympic Committee ever since. It replaced an earlier 'tear-drop' design used from 1994, which itself replaced the original 1988 emblem.

features.symbolism: white as a neutral background showcasing the Agitos symbol

Three crescent shapes (Latin agito, 'I move') in red, blue, and green encircling a central point, representing the role of the International Paralympic Committee in bringing athletes from across the world together. The three colours are the most widely represented in national flags. The design replaced an earlier five-tear-drop emblem (1994) and a still earlier three-tear-drop emblem (1988).
